highlight.title: 2026 Schools White Paper: Major Education Reforms Under Consultation
highlight.summary: The government's 'Every Child Achieving and Thriving' white paper published in February sets out long-term reforms including halving the attainment gap between disadvantaged children and their peers at GCSE level. A consultation on the proposals closes on 18 May 2026.

resources.title: Ofqual Student Guide to Exams and Assessments in 2026
resources.why_now: Essential for both parents and students as GCSE exams run 7 May to 23 June 2026, with A-levels following similar dates - includes key changes like formula sheets being provided for maths, physics and combined science.

resources.title: Oak National Academy Free Teaching Resources
resources.why_now: Perfect for parents supporting home learning and teachers - now includes new SATs preparation materials specifically designed for the May 2026 tests, plus AI tools and comprehensive KS1-KS4 curriculum resources.

resources.title: KS2 SATs 2026: What Parents Need to Know
resources.why_now: Critical timing for Year 6 parents as KS2 SATs are scheduled for 11-14 May 2026 - government guidance emphasises regular attendance is vital in these final weeks, and provides practical support tips for families.

resources.title: Primary Assessment Future Dates 2025-2027
resources.why_now: Essential planning resource for parents with children across Key Stages - includes downloadable calendars with confirmed dates for phonics checks (8 June 2026), multiplication tables check (1 June 2026) and optional KS1 tests.

claude_take: With major education reforms proposed and exam season approaching, families need reliable information more than ever. The government's focus on closing attainment gaps while maintaining rigorous standards creates both opportunities and pressure points that parents should understand early.
